compiled_state.title: brainstorm skill: prompt engineering constraints deferred to implementation -> behavioral failure modes silently introduced
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Component
/dso:brainstorm (epic scrutiny pipeline, Step 5 Prompt Alignment)

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run /dso:brainstorm on an epic that creates new LLM-facing agents
2. Bot-psychologist identifies specific behavioral failure modes and prompt constraints
3. Brainstorm records constraints as "embed during implementation" rather than resolving them into concrete spec language

## Expected Behavior
When the bot-psychologist identifies behavioral failure modes with specific prompt mitigations, brainstorm should resolve those mitigations into testable requirements in the epic spec (e.g., success criteria, agent contract constraints, or explicit SKILL.md instructions) while the user is present to validate the behavioral intent. Prompt engineering constraints require understanding of user intent (e.g., "should the agent err toward removal or caution?") that cannot be recovered during implementation.

## Actual Behavior
Bot-psychologist findings are noted as prose recommendations ("embed as hard constraints in agent definition files during implementation") without being formalized into the epic spec. Implementation agents then write agent prompts without these constraints, introducing the exact failure modes the bot-psychologist identified.

## Impact
The bot-psychologist stage exists precisely because prompt behavioral failures are subtle and non-obvious. Deferring the constraints to implementation defeats the purpose of running the analysis during brainstorm. The user's attention and intent — available during brainstorm — is the scarce resource needed to validate directional choices like "default to DISMISS when uncertain."
